name: Trusted Relationship
description: Adversaries exploit trusted relationships between organizations to gain access to a target. In Azure/Entra ID, this includes compromise of Managed Service Providers (MSPs) with delegated admin access (GDAP/DAP), exploitation of cross-tenant B2B guest accounts, abuse of Entra ID External Identities, or compromise of a supplier whose service principal has permissions in the target tenant. The adversary operates with legitimate credentials that have pre-existing trust, making their access appear authorized in audit logs.
iocs: SigninLogs entries with HomeTenantId different from the ResourceTenantId — cross-tenant B2B authentication
AuditLogs Operation 'Add delegated permission grant' or 'Consent on behalf of organization' initiated by an external tenant identity
SigninLogs UserType == 'Guest' performing administrative operations beyond expected scope
AzureActivity operations initiated by a service principal with a cross-tenant application (HomeTenantId != subscription TenantId)
AuditLogs showing GDAP relationship activation followed immediately by privileged role assignments in the target tenant
SigninLogs CrossTenantAccessType == 'B2BCollaboration' or 'B2BDirectConnect' from an MSP tenant IP for roles like Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator
Bulk AuditLogs operations (user creation, permission grants, policy changes) performed by an external identity in a short time window — MSP compromise
evasion_variants: Operate exclusively within the legitimate scope of the MSP's GDAP permissions to avoid privilege escalation alerts
Perform actions spread across multiple days mimicking normal MSP administrative cadence
Use a B2B guest account that has been dormant and recently reactivated — less likely to have anomaly baseline triggers
Leverage a supplier service principal with existing Reader or Contributor role rather than creating new credentials
Target a non-production subscription via the trusted relationship to establish foothold before pivoting to production
Use the legitimate MSP ticketing system to initiate actions, making the activity appear change-management-authorized
detection_kql_hints: SigninLogs | where HomeTenantId != ResourceTenantId | where ResultType == 0 | summarize count() by UserPrincipalName, HomeTenantId, AppDisplayName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)

AuditLogs | where InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName has '#EXT#' | where OperationName in ('Add member to role','Add delegated permission grant','Reset user password') | project TimeGenerated, OperationName, InitiatedBy, TargetResources
AzureActivity | where Claims has '"idtyp":"app"' | extend AppTenant = tostring(parse_json(Claims).tid) | where AppTenant != TenantId | where OperationNameValue has_any ('WRITE','DELETE','ACTION')

AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Add member to role' | where InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName has 'partner' or InitiatedBy.app.displayName has_any ('GDAP','Lighthouse','Partner') | project TimeGenerated, TargetResources, RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)

SigninLogs | where CrossTenantAccessType in ('B2BCollaboration','B2BDirectConnect') | where UserPrincipalName !in (known_msp_accounts) | where AppDisplayName has_any ('Azure Portal','Microsoft 365 Admin')
